label: Kotlin
description: Primary language for Android and backend JVM services. Used daily in production across coroutines, sealed classes, and extension functions.

label: Python
description: Used for scripting, data processing pipelines, and competitive programming problem-solving utilities.

label: Java
description: Foundation Android language. Maintained legacy codebases and migrated modules to Kotlin interop patterns.

label: TypeScript
description: Used in full-stack and portfolio projects. Strict mode enforced; leverages discriminated unions and utility types.

label: JavaScript
description: ES2020+ features, async/await patterns, and DOM manipulation. Substrate for TypeScript work.

label: Dart
description: Flutter's primary language. Used for async stream handling, null safety patterns, and widget composition.

label: SQL
description: PostgreSQL and SQLite for schema design, query optimization, and migrations in backend and Android (Room) projects.

label: Bash
description: Shell scripting for CI/CD pipeline automation, build tooling, and deployment scripts.

label: Go
description: Used for high-throughput REST microservices and CLI tooling. Leverages goroutines and channel-based concurrency.

label: C++
description: Competitive programming workhorse. STL mastery — maps, sets, priority queues, and custom comparators.

label: Jetpack Compose
description: Declarative Android UI toolkit. Deep familiarity with recomposition lifecycle, state hoisting, custom layouts, and animation APIs.

label: Kotlin Coroutines
description: Structured concurrency for Android and JVM. Scopes, dispatchers, Flow operators, and exception handling in production.

label: MVVM
description: Model-View-ViewModel applied with Android ViewModel, StateFlow, and unidirectional data flow principles.

label: Clean Architecture
description: Layer-based separation into data, domain, and presentation layers. Domain models isolated from framework dependencies.

label: Dependency Injection
description: Hilt on Android and manual DI in Go services. Constructor injection preferred; interface-driven for testability.

label: REST API Design
description: Resource-oriented API design, versioning strategies, HTTP semantics, pagination, and OpenAPI documentation.

label: WebSockets
description: Full-duplex communication for real-time features. Handled connection lifecycle, reconnection strategies, and heartbeat protocols.

label: RxJava
description: Reactive extensions used in legacy Android codebases. Observable chains, schedulers, and backpressure handling.

label: Unit Testing
description: JUnit5, MockK for Kotlin, and Vitest for TypeScript. Test pyramid adherence with emphasis on domain layer isolation.

label: CI/CD
description: GitHub Actions pipelines for build, test, and deploy workflows. Gradle build caching and Docker-based deployment stages.

label: Graph Algorithms
description: BFS, DFS, Dijkstra, Bellman-Ford, topological sort, and union-find. Applied in both contest and system design contexts.

label: Dynamic Programming
description: Top-down memoization and bottom-up tabulation. Knapsack, LCS, matrix chain, digit DP, and bitmask DP patterns.

label: System Design
description: Distributed systems fundamentals: CAP theorem, load balancing, caching strategies, message queues, and API gateway patterns.

label: State Management
description: Redux/Zustand for web and StateFlow/MVI for Android. Predictable state machines with immutable state transitions.

label: Android Development
description: End-to-end Android application development: architecture, lifecycle management, performance profiling, and Play Store deployment.

label: Backend Development
description: REST microservices, authentication flows, database integration, and horizontal scaling strategies.

label: Competitive Programming
description: Codeforces and LeetCode competitor. 340+ problems solved. Strong in DP, graphs, and number theory.

label: Data Structures & Algorithms
description: Segment trees, tries, heaps, balanced BSTs, and advanced graph structures applied in both competitive and production contexts.

label: UI/UX Design
description: Design systems, component libraries, motion design, and accessibility principles. Figma for prototyping.

label: DevOps
description: Docker, container orchestration basics, environment parity, and infrastructure-as-code with lightweight configs.

label: Flutter
description: Cross-platform mobile development. Widget tree optimization, custom painters, and platform channel integration.

label: Firebase
description: Firestore, Authentication, Cloud Messaging, and Crashlytics. Real-time listeners and offline persistence patterns.

label: Database Design
description: Relational schema normalization, index strategy, query planning, and migration management with golang-migrate.

label: Machine Learning
description: Supervised learning fundamentals, scikit-learn pipelines, and TensorFlow Lite model integration in Android apps.

label: React
description: Component architecture, hooks, concurrent rendering, and performance optimizations via memoization and virtualization.
